message: fix(crates): clarify rationale for renamed package + lib alias

files.patch:
@@ -9,12 +9,12 @@ keywords = ["cli", "agent", "session", "resume", "claude"]
 categories = ["command-line-utilities"]
 readme = "README.md"
 
-# The package was published on crates.io as `casr` early on, and the binary
-# plus all internal `casr::...` crate-path references throughout main.rs/tests
-# remain on that short name. The `casr` crates.io slot ended up taken by an
-# unrelated project, so we publish the package itself under the longer name
-# while keeping `casr` as both the lib name (for `casr::*` paths) and the
-# binary entry point.
+# Originally intended to publish on crates.io as `casr`, but that short
+# name is held by an unrelated project (anfedotoff's CASR crash analyzer),
+# so the package itself goes out under the longer canonical name. All the
+# internal `casr::...` crate-path references throughout main.rs/tests/etc
+# stay on the short name because we keep `casr` as both the lib name (for
+# `casr::*` paths) and the binary entry point.
 [lib]
 name = "casr"
 path = "src/lib.rs"
